title: [Bug]: NATS token authentication can be combined with username and password
summary: The configuration model allows token authentication fields to be combined with username and password fields.
observed: A configuration can select token authentication and username/password authentication at the same time.
expected: NATS authentication modes must be mutually exclusive so operators choose one clear mode.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that combines token and username/password authentication fields.
impact: Ambiguous client authentication can fail unpredictably or hide which credential source is actually used.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. The fix blocks ambiguous authentication before connecting to NATS.
security: The fix follows least surprise and least credential exposure by requiring one authentication method.
acceptance: Token authentication cannot be combined with username/password authentication.
Standalone token authentication continues to work.
Standalone username/password authentication continues to work.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, NATS connection option tests, configuration tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update NATS authentication documentation if mutual exclusivity is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
